findings.title: Empty / uninstrumented signup funnel is the #1 blocker to the 100/day north star
findings.founder_problem: There is no measurable acquisition happening. Without instrumented top-of-funnel events and a public value moment, no experiment can be evaluated against the 100/day target.
findings.investor_problem: Investor side is not being acquired at all; no investor signup operations recorded.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: signup_velocity.signups_per_day=0, gap_to_target=100, pct_of_target=0; ai_logs had 0 of [url_submitted, login_completed, lookup_signup_completed, checkout_completed] in 7d; internal_events.sampled=0.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Ship a public, pre-signup 'instant investor match preview' so a visitor gets value (a ranked shortlist + one thesis-fit reason) BEFORE creating an account, then instrument every funnel stage. This is the single lever that converts the 0 baseline into measurable signups.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Public URL-submission -> instant match preview -> account-create CTA. Instrument awareness/value_preview/account_created events in ai_logs.
findings.status_update: 2026-06-27: Instrumentation shipped — 979+ url_submitted/7d, heartbeat 15/17 stages, founder_demand_events live. Superseded by F-2026-0624-14 (conversion leak).

handoff.title: Public instant investor-match preview (value before signup) + funnel instrumentation

findings.title: Investors are drowning in 'vibe-coded slop' — dealflow volume is now a vanity metric
findings.founder_problem: Quality founders are buried under noise and struggle to stand out / earn investor trust.
findings.investor_problem: AI lets software startups fabricate credibility in an afternoon; investors face a surge of low-signal submissions and fewer seed startups graduating to Series A, so they concentrate capital in fewer, stronger bets and need better filtering.

evidence.rss: Sifted (2026-06): 'Investors bemoan vibe-coded product slop'; Crunchbase News: 'AI Is Rewriting What Investors Should Look For In Early Startup Teams'.
evidence.web: Crunchbase/seed reports 2026: 'dealflow volume has become a vanity metric... software startups can fabricate credibility in a single afternoon'; fewer seed->Series A graduations; capital concentrating.

findings.pythh_opportunity: A data-completeness + quality/traction signal score on every startup profile, powering a curated weekly dealflow digest for investors. Opens the investor side of the marketplace (currently 0 acquisition).
findings.suggested_product_shape: Profile 'data completeness score' + signal filter; investor-facing curated digest of high-signal matches.

handoff.title: Data-completeness/quality score + curated investor dealflow digest

findings.title: Founders burn ~6 weeks on a spreadsheet + a month cold-emailing wrong-fit investors
findings.founder_problem: Most founders spend more time finding investors than talking to them: ~6 weeks building a list, ~1 month emailing people who were never going to say yes, with work split across disconnected tools (inboxes, sheets, notes, calendars).
findings.investor_problem: Mis-targeted cold outreach adds to inbound noise (links to F-2026-0622-02).

evidence.web: seedlegals/thevccorner 2026: 'most founders spend more time looking for investors than talking to them... six weeks building a spreadsheet, another month cold emailing people who were never going to say yes'; 'work split across too many disconnected tools'.
evidence.rss: Sifted (2026-06): fintech founders crowdfunded EUR8m in <1hr — founders routing around painful VC outreach via alternative channels.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Pythh's core wedge: instant match preview + 'why this VC' thesis-fit explanation that replaces the spreadsheet-and-cold-email grind with a ranked, reasoned shortlist in minutes.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Match preview returns ranked investors each with a one-line thesis-fit rationale and suggested approach.

handoff.title: Thesis-fit explainer ('why this VC') on every match

findings.title: Targeting must be partner-level, not firm-level — a data gap competitors leave open
findings.founder_problem: Every partner has distinct tastes and convictions; founders waste meetings pitching firms instead of the one aligned partner, and lack partner-level data to do it.
findings.investor_problem: Individual partners get pitched on themes outside their conviction.

evidence.web: thevccorner 2026: 'partner targeting — not firm targeting'; seedblink 'what investors expect from founders in 2026'.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Resolve and score thesis fit at partner granularity (not firm), a differentiator vs firm-level databases (PitchBook, Harmonic). Feeds the match preview and completeness score.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Partner-level profiles with thesis/stage/check-size completeness; match at partner level.

findings.title: Fresh seed dry powder + bifurcated AI-winner market = deployment-timing matching opportunity
findings.founder_problem: In a bifurcated, AI-winner-take-most market founders must time outreach to funds that just raised / are actively deploying, and broaden capital sources.
findings.investor_problem: Newly-raised funds (e.g. Seedcamp $320M) need quality seed dealflow to deploy.

evidence.rss: TechCrunch/Crunchbase/Sifted (2026-06): 'Seedcamp raises $320M new fund'; Crunchbase 'Robotics startups on fire... record numbers 2026'.
evidence.web: Crunchbase: Q1 2026 record ~$297B venture, 81% to AI; Morgan Stanley: >half of founders pursuing broader range of capital sources.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Investor deployment-timing alerts ('this fund just closed / is actively deploying in your sector') for founders, and broader-capital-source matching. Recurring alerts create a retention loop.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Timing signal on investor profiles ('recently raised / actively deploying') surfaced in matches + alert subscription.

findings.title: Valuation/terms uncertainty in a frothy-but-cautious market — founders don't know what to ask, investors lack live comps
findings.founder_problem: Founders entering a round have no trustworthy, sector/stage-specific reference for what valuation, check size, or terms (SAFE cap, dilution) to expect — so they over/under-ask, stall, or get washed out in negotiation.
findings.investor_problem: Investors want defensible, current comps to anchor offers; firm-level databases lag and headline mega-rounds distort expectations.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: crunchbase_news friction hit category=pricing_terms on 'AppsFlyer Reportedly Lands $1B At $2.7B Valuation'; Crunchbase News: 'Greenspan Penned Irrational Exuberance 30 Years Ago. It Aged Well.' (valuation-frothiness caution); Q1 2026 record ~$297B venture with 81% to AI = bifurcated, distorted pricing.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary); pricing_terms is a recurring friction category with a live RSS hit today

findings.pythh_opportunity: Add a valuation/terms benchmark to the match preview: 'comparable raises at your stage/sector look like X' plus a thesis-aligned check-size expectation per matched investor. Appeals to both sides and deepens the value moment before signup.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Stage/sector terms benchmark widget in match preview + per-investor expected check size; sourced from internal startup_events + funding RSS.
findings.status_update: 2026-06-24: corroborated by a cluster of mega-round froth signals — Sifted 'Records broken as eight European startups raised $1bn+ rounds in 2026', Crunchbase News 'Menlo Ventures Raises $3B In New Funds', and the recurring 'Greenspan Penned Irrational Exuberance 30 Years Ago. It Aged Well.' These headlines actively distort what a typical seed founder thinks their round should look like. Sharpens the opportunity: a 'valuation reality-check' widget in the preview ('mega-rounds aren't your round — comparable seed raises in your sector look like X') doubles as a TRUST signal (picky/skeptical voice), not just data. Promote toward backlog.

findings.title: 'Services-as-software' shift — position Pythh as an agent that does the fundraising work, not another database
findings.founder_problem: Founders are tired of buying yet another data tool they must operate manually (lists, CRMs, spreadsheets); they want the work done for them.
findings.investor_problem: Investors equally want curated output (a digest, a shortlist) rather than another dashboard to maintain.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: crunchbase_news 'Saas Isn't Coming Back. Something Much Bigger Is Replacing It' (services-as-software / agentic shift); venturebeat cluster on agentic enterprises and self-improving agents.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external); positioning/messaging signal rather than a discrete feature gap

findings.pythh_opportunity: Frame Pythh's wedge (instant match preview + thesis-fit explainer + curated digest) as agentic 'fundraising-as-a-service' in landing copy and onboarding — differentiator vs database competitors (PitchBook, Harmonic, Affinity) and an awareness lever.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Messaging/positioning experiment for Growth Agent: 'Pythh does the fundraising legwork' landing variant; measure value_preview->account_created lift.

findings.title: Founders are routing around VC — matching to the right capital TYPE (not just VCs) broadens Pythh's TAM
findings.founder_problem: Many founders are not VC-fit (wrong stage, sector, growth profile) yet still need capital. They increasingly bypass the VC grind via crowdfunding, angels, revenue-based finance, and strategics — but have no single place that tells them which capital TYPE fits them and routes them to it.
findings.investor_problem: Non-VC capital providers (syndicates, crowdfunding platforms, RBF, angels) also lack a quality, pre-qualified founder pipeline.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: Sifted (Jun 2026) 'How these fintech founders crowdfunded EUR8m in less than an hour' — founders raising fast outside traditional VC outreach.
evidence.web: Morgan Stanley 2026: >half of founders pursuing a broader range of capital sources; prior-cycle corroboration.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary); recurring 'broader capital sources' theme across multiple cycles

findings.pythh_opportunity: Add a capital-type router to the match preview: classify the founder and surface the best-fit capital TYPE (VC / angel / crowdfunding / RBF / strategic) alongside specific matches. Captures the large segment of founders who would bounce off a VC-only product, widening top-of-funnel without new acquisition spend.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Match preview returns a 'best capital fit' verdict + routes (VC shortlist, or angel/crowdfunding/RBF alternatives) instead of VC-only results.

handoff.title: Capital-type router in match preview (VC vs angel/crowdfunding/RBF/strategic)

findings.title: Capital is rotating fast between hot sectors — a live 'is your sector hot / who's deploying' signal sharpens the value moment
findings.founder_problem: Founders can't see, in the moment, whether capital is flowing into their sector this quarter or which investors are actively writing checks there — so they mistime outreach and pitch funds that have rotated away.
findings.investor_problem: Investors want to spot where momentum is concentrating (and where it is cooling) without manually tracking round-by-round news.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: Crunchbase News 'Robotics Startups On Fire As Venture Funding Surges To Record Numbers In 2026'; 'World-Model Startup Odyssey Leads With $310M In Slower Week For Large Deals' (rotation + cooling in same week); ~81% of Q1 2026 venture to AI.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external); distinct from F-05 (fund-level deploy timing) — this is sector-level demand heat

findings.pythh_opportunity: A sector-heat signal on the match preview: 'Your sector is hot right now — N matched investors made a check here in the last 90 days' (or a cooling warning). Cheap to compute from funding RSS + internal startup_events; deepens the pre-signup value moment for both audiences and gives investors a reason to return.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Sector-heat badge in preview (heating/cooling + count of recently-active matched investors), sourced from funding RSS + internal startup_events.

findings.title: Internal founder-side inventory is empty (startup_events = 0) — the match engine has nothing to match
findings.founder_problem: No founder/startup events are being captured, so even a perfect match preview has no live demand-side data to learn from or personalize against.
findings.investor_problem: Without a populated founder/startup inventory there is no dealflow to curate or digest for investors — the investor side cannot launch.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-22T22:20Z: internal_events.sampled=0, friction_hits=0; startup_events returned 0 rows over the 7-day window. Pairs with F-01 (uninstrumented funnel).
evidence.strength: validated (internal data, strongest evidence) — this is a confirmed supply gap, not a hypothesis

findings.status_update: 2026-06-27: ADDRESSED — founder_demand_events table shipped; 123+ events/7d at deploy verify. preview + url capture wired in instantSubmit + previewRoute. Monitor founder_demand.events_7d in conversion:funnel.
findings.pythh_opportunity: Seed and instrument the founder-supply layer: capture every URL submission / preview request as a startup_event so the match engine and dealflow digest have inventory. This is the data prerequisite that makes instant_match_preview and dealflow_quality_score actually work end-to-end.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Log startup_events on every public preview request (URL, derived profile, sector, stage); backfill from existing sources so matching + digest have a corpus.
findings.signup_lever: conversion (enables match quality) + investor retention (enables digest)

handoff.title: Capture startup_events on every preview request to populate match-engine inventory (979 lost records/7d)

findings.title: Preview->signup conversion is 0% — 29 URL submissions in 7d converted to zero accounts
findings.founder_problem: Founders are reaching the value moment (submitting a URL to get a match preview) but none create an account afterward. Either the preview never returns enough value to justify signup, the signup gate appears before value is delivered, or the CTA/flow is broken. The intent exists; the conversion does not.
findings.investor_problem: Investor side still has zero acquisition; a leaking founder funnel means no founder inventory to ever surface to investors.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-23T03:57Z: signup_velocity.url_submitted=29 over 7d (~4.1/day) but signups_total=0, signups_per_day=0, pct_of_target=0. by_operation shows ONLY url_submitted firing — no login_completed, lookup_signup_completed, or checkout_completed. This is a sharp change vs the 2026-06-22 cycle where url_submitted was 0; traffic now exists and the leak is fully localized to preview->account_created.
evidence.strength: validated (internal funnel data, strongest evidence) — measured 100% drop-off at one stage

findings.status_update: 2026-06-27: ADDRESSED — preview→signup no longer 0%; evidence strip + intro CTAs shipped. Binding constraint is now preview→started rate (~2–6%). Tracked in F-2026-0624-14.
findings.pythh_opportunity: Fix the single highest-leverage stage on the path to 100/day: deliver a complete, compelling preview (ranked shortlist + thesis-fit reason) and place the account gate AFTER the value reveal, with a one-tap signup CTA. Converting even 25% of the existing 4.1 submissions/day is the first real signup baseline at zero acquisition cost.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Audit the url_submitted -> preview -> CTA flow; ungate the preview, gate save/intro/export behind signup; instrument value_preview and account_created events so the leak is visible per-stage. A/B the gate position.

handoff.title: Close the preview->signup leak (29 submissions/7d, 0 accounts) — ungate value, gate save/intro, instrument per-stage

findings.title: AI-driven layoff wave is minting a cohort of first-time founders who need exactly Pythh's hand-holding
findings.founder_problem: A surge of operators displaced by AI-cited layoffs are starting companies for the first time. They have no investor network, no warm intros, and no idea who fits their thesis/stage — the precise pain Pythh removes — but they don't yet know a tool like Pythh exists.
findings.investor_problem: Investors must sift a larger, noisier wave of brand-new, unproven founders (compounds dealflow noise in F-02).

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-23: TechCrunch lead story 'The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI'. Pairs with Sifted 'Want to build European AI muscles? Scrap non-compete clauses' (policy easing founder formation).
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary) — macro acquisition-timing signal, not a feature gap

findings.pythh_opportunity: Acquisition timing + targeted awareness: position the free instant match preview at displaced-operator / first-time-founder channels (layoff communities, 'I just got laid off, now building' threads). This cohort is large, motivated, and underserved by relationship-gated incumbents (warm-intro-only networks).
findings.suggested_product_shape: Growth Agent awareness experiment: 'first-time founder, no investor network? get your investor shortlist in 60s' landing variant aimed at the laid-off-builder segment.

findings.title: Proof-of-legitimacy gap: AI fabricates credibility, so a verified-signal layer is what cuts through the slop
findings.founder_problem: When anyone can vibe-code a credible-looking product in an afternoon, genuine founders with real traction can't visibly distinguish themselves from fabricated ones — their authentic signal is lost in the noise.
findings.investor_problem: Investors can no longer trust surface credibility; they need verified, hard-to-fake signals (real usage, real revenue, real team provenance) to filter, and security incidents (e.g. mass-exploited Langflow servers) raise the bar on trusting any self-reported claim.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-23: Sifted 'Investors bemoan vibe-coded product slop'; VentureBeat '7,000 Langflow servers are under attack' (self-reported tech claims are unreliable). Extends F-02 (dealflow noise) with a distinct VERIFICATION angle rather than a completeness-score angle.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary) — distinct verification framing of the F-02 noise problem

findings.pythh_opportunity: Layer a verification/provenance signal on top of the F-02 data-completeness score: connect-to-verify hooks (analytics, payment processor, domain/code provenance) that turn a profile from 'claimed' to 'verified'. Verified founders convert better (they want the badge); investors get a hard-to-fake filter — a two-sided reason to sign up.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Optional 'verify your traction' step (OAuth to analytics/billing) producing a verified badge on the profile; investor digest can filter to verified-only. Pairs with dealflow_quality_score.

handoff.title: Verified-traction/provenance layer on profiles (connect-to-verify badge + investor verified-only filter)

findings.title: Funnel inflected: 140 URL/day inbound but only 2.1% sign up — the binding constraint is now a leaky bucket, not an empty one
findings.founder_problem: Founders are arriving and hitting the value moment at scale (~140 URL submissions/day, far above the 100/day signup target if they converted), but ~98% leave without creating an account. The intent is overwhelming; the conversion is not. This is the single highest-leverage number on the path to 100/day: at 2.1% we get 3 signups/day; at 15% the same traffic yields 21/day; at 50% it clears the north star outright with zero new acquisition spend.
findings.investor_problem: Investor-side acquisition is still 0 (no investor signup operations), so a leaking founder funnel is the only fuel — and it is leaking at 98%.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-24T23:25Z: signup_velocity.url_submitted=979 over 7d (~139.9/day), signups_total=21 (~3/day), pct_of_target=3, gap_to_target=97. by_operation: checkout_completed=10, lookup_signup_completed=11, url_submitted=979. orchestrator-brief 2026-06-24: signup_per_preview=2.4, founder_preview_to_started=46.2, founder_started_to_completed=166.7, complete_per_checkout=100. So once a founder STARTS signup they finish (and 10 reached paid checkout this window — the first validated revenue path), but the preview->started step is where ~98% are lost.
evidence.strength: validated (internal funnel data, strongest evidence) — measured conversion ceiling

findings.pythh_opportunity: Attack the preview->started step, not acquisition. Two validated facts make this tractable: (1) traffic is abundant (140/day), (2) founders who START signup convert almost fully (46.2% preview->started but 166.7% started->completed implies near-zero abandonment once committed) and 10 even reached paid checkout. So the lever is purely: get more previewers to START. Deliver a complete, evidence-backed preview (ranked shortlist + one thesis-fit 'why' with hard evidence) and gate only save/intro/export behind the account — a one-tap signup CTA placed AFTER the value reveal.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Preview-cliffhanger v2: ungated ranked shortlist + 1 explain block visible immediately; account gate moves behind save/intro/export with one-tap signup. A/B the gate position; instrument value_preview and account_started per-stage so the 2.1% is decomposable.

handoff.title: Lift preview->signup from 2.1% — ungate the value reveal, gate save/intro/export, one-tap CTA, per-stage instrumentation

findings.title: AI-tool ROI distrust is now active buying behavior — the preview must PROVE value before it asks for anything
findings.founder_problem: Buyers have become reflexively skeptical of AI tools that burn time or money without proving value. A founder landing on Pythh now arrives pre-loaded with 'is this another AI tool that wastes my afternoon?' distrust — so any signup/cost ask BEFORE a concrete, evidence-backed result triggers a bounce. This is a plausible mechanism behind the 98% preview drop-off in F-14.
findings.investor_problem: Investors apply the same ROI-skepticism lens to dealflow tools; they won't adopt a digest unless its first output is verifiably high-signal.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-24: TechCrunch 'Companies are scrambling to stop employees from maxing out AI budgets with small tasks' (active distrust of AI spend without ROI) and 'New website names and shames companies that still don't offer passkeys' (public trust/accountability pressure). Pairs with the established F-13 'vibe-coded slop' verification theme.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary) proposing a CAUSAL mechanism for the validated F-14 drop-off

findings.pythh_opportunity: Lean into the picky/skeptical voice as a conversion asset: open the preview with proof and filtering ('47 investors screened out, 3 fit — here's the evidence'), never with a gate or a hype claim. Showing what DOESN'T fit, with hard evidence (recent check, stage, check size, portfolio overlap), is exactly the antidote to AI-tool distrust and is on-brand per the orchestrator voice mandate. This is a messaging+sequencing change, not new infra.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Preview copy/sequence experiment: evidence-and-rejection-first reveal ('N screened out, M fit, here's why') ahead of any account/cost ask; measure value_preview->account_started lift vs current.

handoff.title: Evidence-and-rejection-first preview reveal (skeptic-proof the value moment before any ask)

findings.title: 979 one-shot previews, zero return trigger — Pythh has no habit loop while competitors retain via recurring digests
findings.founder_problem: A founder gets a one-shot preview and has no reason to ever come back: fundraising is episodic, and a static shortlist goes stale. Competitors create stickiness with recurring, account-anchored loops (Visible's investor-update cadence, Affinity/Carta's CRM-of-record gravity) that pull users back weekly. Pythh's value evaporates after one visit.
findings.investor_problem: Investors won't form a daily/weekly habit around a tool that doesn't deliver fresh, changing signal; without a return trigger the investor side can't retain even if it acquires.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-24: url_submitted=979/7d with signups_total=21 and intro_per_match_view=0 (orchestrator-brief) — high one-shot volume, no measured downstream return action. north-star engagement_loops list (signal_delta, investor_movement_digest) are defined but unbuilt.
evidence.rss: Sifted 2026-06 'Records broken as eight European startups raised $1bn+ rounds in 2026' + Crunchbase 'Menlo Ventures Raises $3B' = constant fund/round movement that a delta-digest could surface as a recurring reason to return.
evidence.strength: validated supply signal (one-shot volume) + hypothesis on the retention remedy

findings.pythh_opportunity: Ship a Signal-Delta Digest: a weekly trigger that re-engages every previewer/signup with what CHANGED — 'N thesis-fit investors became active in your sector this week', 'your GOD score moved +X', 'a matched fund just raised'. This converts a one-shot tool into a habit loop (north-star loops signal_delta + investor_movement_digest) and is the only finding here that targets retention rather than the front door.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Weekly delta email/push keyed to each saved profile's sector+stage, sourced from funding RSS + internal startup_events; in-app 'what changed' badge. Requires the F-10 startup_events corpus to be non-empty to personalize.

handoff.title: Signal-Delta weekly digest (turn one-shot previews into a return loop) — keyed to sector/stage changes

findings.title: Marketplace is going one-sided — founder acquisition inflected to 140/day while investor signups stay at 0
findings.founder_problem: If only founders sign up, the intro/match loop has no one on the other end — founders' intro requests will dead-end, eroding the trust the preview just built.
findings.investor_problem: Investors are not being acquired at all (every signup operation this window is founder-side: lookup_signup_completed=11, checkout_completed=10, no investor op). The investor-facing dealflow digest — the natural investor wedge — has no audience and, per F-10, no curated corpus to send. The two-sided marketplace is forming a 1-sided imbalance precisely as founder volume scales.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-24: signup_velocity.by_operation contains only founder-side ops (lookup_signup_completed=11, checkout_completed=10, url_submitted=979); zero investor signup operations. competitor_watch (Harmonic, PitchBook, Affinity, Visible) all monetize the investor side Pythh hasn't opened.
evidence.strength: validated (internal funnel data) — confirmed absence of investor-side acquisition

findings.pythh_opportunity: Use the now-abundant founder inventory as the bait to open the investor side: a curated, outbound 'Weekly Dealflow Digest' (top high-signal founders matched to a named investor's thesis) sent cold to a seed list of target investors. This is an ACTIVE outbound loop that seeds investor acquisition from existing founder supply — no new founder-side cost. Depends on F-10 (populate startup_events) to have anything to curate.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Outbound investor digest MVP: hand-curate 5 high-signal founders -> match to 20 target investors' theses -> cold send with a one-tap 'see your full thesis-matched dealflow' signup link; measure investor url_submitted/signup from ref.
findings.signup_lever: investor awareness + acquisition (opens the second side)

handoff.title: Outbound Weekly Dealflow Digest to seed investor-side acquisition from existing founder supply
